analysis.summary: Training failed before completing epoch 1 because DataLoader workers exhausted shared memory.
findings: The run created initial config and dataframe artifacts but produced no checkpoints or evaluation metrics.
The failure occurred at the first training batch with DataLoader worker bus errors.
The active dataloader settings were num_workers=24 and prefetch_factor=10, which are high shared-memory pressure for 512px batches.
analysis.hypothesis: The baseline model/training settings remain untested; the failure is caused by dataloader shared-memory pressure rather than model quality.
analysis.justification: Reducing dataloader concurrency should allow the same baseline hypothesis to run without changing the model, loss, scheduler, or data.

next_action.reason: Retry the baseline with lower dataloader shared-memory pressure after worker bus errors.
